faqs.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faqs.subtitle: Find answers to common questions about FileReduce
faqs.contactDescription: Feel free to reach out to us via WhatsApp or email for support, suggestions, or inquiries.

general.title: General
general.a1: FileReduce is a dynamic EDIFACT/JSONL processing tool that converts EDIFACT files to structured JSONL and compresses them using the .fra format for significant storage savings.
general.a2: FileReduce uses a specialized compression algorithm that achieves 95%+ savings by removing redundancy in JSONL structures while maintaining lossless restoration capabilities.
general.a3: Yes, all processing happens locally in your browser using WebAssembly. No files are sent to external servers unless you explicitly use the backend API option.

usage.title: Usage
usage.a1: FileReduce supports EDIFACT (.edi, .edifact), JSONL (.jsonl, .json), and .fra compression files.
usage.a2: Yes, FileReduce can handle files up to several hundred megabytes using WebAssembly local processing or backend API for even larger files.
usage.a3: Go to the Compression page, upload your .fra file, and click 'Decompress to JSONL'. The original JSONL will be restored losslessly.

technical.title: Technical
technical.a1: .fra is a custom compression format optimized for JSONL data, achieving extreme compression ratios by leveraging structural patterns in EDIFACT-derived JSONL.
technical.q2: Does FileReduce support all EDIFACT versions?
technical.a2: FileReduce automatically detects EDIFACT versions and can generate translation dictionaries on-the-fly using its built‑in scraper for edifactory.de.
technical.a3: Yes, FileReduce provides a REST API and WebAssembly module for integration into your own applications.

about.title: About FileReduce
about.subtitle: Transforming EDIFACT processing with dynamic translations and extreme compression
about.mission: To simplify and optimize EDIFACT data processing through automation, compression, and user‑friendly interfaces.
about.vision: A world where EDIFACT data exchange is as simple as dragging and dropping a file, with intelligent compression reducing storage costs by 95%+.

features.dynamic: Dynamic translation dictionaries that adapt to any EDIFACT version
features.local: Local browser processing via WebAssembly for privacy and speed
features.zeroConfig: Zero‑config automatic version detection and dictionary generation

about.contactDescription: We welcome feedback, suggestions, and collaboration opportunities.
about.story1: FileReduce started as a solution to a common problem in EDI processing: static, hard‑coded translation tables that couldn't adapt to new EDIFACT versions. By introducing dynamic translation dictionaries and automatic version detection, we eliminated the need for manual updates and configuration.
about.story2: The second breakthrough came with the .fra compression format. While working with large JSONL datasets derived from EDIFACT, we noticed extreme redundancy in the structure. By developing a specialized compression algorithm tailored to this pattern, we achieved consistent savings of 95% or more.
about.story3: Today, FileReduce combines these innovations with a modern web interface, WebAssembly for local processing, and a zero‑config architecture that makes EDIFACT processing accessible to everyone—from logistics managers to data engineers.

generate.title: EDIFACT Test File Generator
generate.description: Generate synthetic EDIFACT files of any version and size (1-200MB) for testing performance and compression.
generate.sizeHint: Larger files will take longer to generate and may impact browser performance.
generate.readyDescription: Your EDIFACT test file has been generated. Click the link below to download.
generate.downloadHint: File will be automatically removed from memory after download.
generate.noteText: Generating files larger than 50MB may cause browser slowdowns or crashes. Consider using smaller sizes for testing. The generated EDIFACT uses a simple synthetic structure and may not be semantically valid for all versions.
